behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: dispatcher -> three reviewer clients with role-specific payloads. This is not one local function only, so Unit is too low. Integration is sufficient with in-process reviewer fakes that assert each outbound payload. Rejected higher level: behavior text is explicitly about "sends ... correct ... instruction pack" (plumbing), not model-output quality, so Agentic is disqualified; no real OS/network requirement is stated, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Wire dispatcher with three fake reviewer adapters, submit one contract, and assert each adapter received the identical source document plus the correct role-specific instruction pack (extractor/risk/compliance) exactly once.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on live model judgment over legal text. A stubbed extractor would hide failures in term identification quality (MOCK-HIDE), so Integration is too low. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic decision point (extractor), not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model on a curated contract set with gold annotations for parties/dates/obligations/penalties; assert required recall/precision thresholds and verify key terms are present in output.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is invocation plumbing correctness (config propagation) across modules, not model decision quality. Unit is too low because boundary is caller -> assessor client. Rejected Agentic by behavior text "configuration is passed correctly" (transport/serialization only). Rejected System because in-process fake assessor can catch payload-shape/value errors.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke risk-review path with a non-default risk policy and capture outbound request in a fake assessor client; assert policy fields, versions, and thresholds match expected serialized payload.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model semantic interpretation, especially indirect/euphemistic phrasing. Mocked outputs would always pass expected flags and hide true failures. Rejected Workflow: this behavior targets one reviewer’s decision quality, not multi-agent sequential reasoning.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk assessor on policy-labeled contract snippets including explicit and euphemistic risky language; assert detection quality against labeled outcomes (e.g., minimum recall on euphemistic cases).

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about parameter handoff to compliance invocation, a cross-module wiring concern. Unit is too low if serialization/mapping occurs across components. Rejected Agentic because behavior text scopes to "are passed correctly" rather than correctness of compliance judgment. Rejected System: fake adapter can validate payload without real network/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute compliance-dispatch with specific jurisdiction/regulatory profile inputs and assert fake compliance client receives exact mapped values (including defaults/overrides) in request payload.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: This tests substantive compliance determination quality from a live AI reviewer. Deterministic stubs cannot reveal misclassification against regulatory requirements. Rejected Workflow: single reviewer quality check; no required multi-agent sequence in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real compliance checker on contracts with expert-labeled pass/fail outcomes per regulatory profile; assert agreement/accuracy thresholds and verify requirement-level justifications are consistent with labels.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Failure is primarily deterministic merge logic on input records; one local merge function/object can reproduce and catch it. No real infra or model output needed. Rejected Integration: behavior text focuses on merge rule "preserves both assessments with attribution," which can be validated with direct function inputs without module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge logic with synthetic reviewer outputs containing same-clause conflicting labels and assert unified report contains both assessments and correct reviewer attribution metadata.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is a full pipeline with multiple reviewer calls and merge, and reviewers are AI components on real contract input. Multiple agentic steps are sequenced and their handoff/aggregation must be validated together. Rejected lower levels: Integration/Agentic single-step tests cannot expose cross-step loss of findings across dispatch -> collect -> merge.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full pipeline on real contracts using live extractor/risk/compliance reviewers; assert final unified report contains all findings produced by each reviewer (no dropped items) and includes merged conflict entries when applicable.
